mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rate-cut periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 8.0 percent, it signals a tightening labor market that boosts household disposable income and consumption demand, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative or flat 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses below 8.0 percent within a 3-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in industrial production and housing starts indicate synchronized real-economy expansion, which validates corporate revenue growth expectations and lifts equity valuations over a 20-day forward window.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces a negative directional accuracy below 50% over a 3-year out-of-sample test, or the interaction term coefficient in a multivariate regression is statistically indistinguishable from zero (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: During a post-shock recovery phase, quarterly real GDP growth exceeding 2.5 percent annualized rate confirms structural demand restoration, leading to sustained positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon as earnings revisions turn upward.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns average below zero when GDPC1 quarterly growth exceeds 2.5 percent annualized during the specified regime, or the signal's information ratio drops below 0.5 over a 5-year walk-forward period.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month CPI index increase exceeding 0.4 percent triggers immediate repricing of inflation expectations, compressing equity risk premiums and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 60% of instances following a CPI monthly jump above 0.4 percent, or the signal's hit rate fails to exceed 55% over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Steady month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls above 150,000 persons sustains aggregate consumption demand, which supports corporate earnings trajectories and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The rolling 3-year directional accuracy of the signal falls below 52%, or forward 60-day returns in months with payroll growth above 150,000 are statistically indistinguishable from zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in the unemployment rate and increases in retail and food services sales indicate a self-reinforcing consumption cycle, which accelerates earnings revision momentum and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to produce a positive hit rate above 55% over a 2-year out-of-sample test, or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for FEDFUNDS changes in a multivariate model.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion regime, a month-over-month decline in the industrial production index signals supply chain bottlenecks and demand saturation, which triggers immediate equity risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when INDPRO contracts during the specified regime, or the signal's Sharpe ratio over a 3-year walk-forward period falls below 0.2.
